sections.title: Pre-contract and contractual disclosures for consumer products
sections.summary: Institutions must disclose all terms, conditions, features, risks, benefits, fees, and charges to consumers before and during contract execution. They must provide a Key Facts Statement (KFS) for loans, allow a 3-day cooling-off period after signing, and ensure variations in interest rates are notified with specific details. These disclosures apply to credit, deposit, and other financial products to ensure transparency and informed consumer choices.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed pre-contract disclosures for financial products, including key terms, fees, and interest rates, which impact data transfer impact assessments by mandating clear communication of financial terms before data processing begins.

requirements.text: Disclose all terms and conditions of a product or service on offer, as well as features, risks, benefits, fees and associated charges
Disclose in contracts the possibility of variations in interest rates or foreign exchange due to market conditions
Disclose similar products and services within the Institution to enable consumer comparisons
Provide a Key Facts Statement (KFS) in the format attached as Annexure A stating a summary of key information on consumer loans
Allow consumers a minimum of two (2) days from date of receipt to review draft contract before execution
Give a consumer a cooling-off period of three (3) working days after signing a credit contract to cancel without penalty
Notify consumers of any transaction on their account immediately it is made, including date, amount, payer, transaction reference, channel and cleared account balance
Ensure descriptions in account statements are clear, including transaction reference, payer, transaction channel, location and account balance
Ensure credit contracts contain key features and risks, tenor of the contract, interest/fee calculations, APR, notice of interest rate variations, and complaint escalation channels
Provide monthly statements for all products and account types at no cost
Give prior notice of interest rate variations to customers, stating reason, commencement date, revised repayment schedule, and alternatives

sections.source_quote: Institutions shall comply with the following requirements prior to the execution of any contract with consumers: Disclose all terms and conditions of a product or service on offer, as well as the features, risks, benefits, fees and other associated charges.

sections.title: Responsible business conduct and data protection for consumers
sections.summary: Institutions must conduct business ethically, train staff, provide clear product information, and ensure consumers know complaint channels. They must display all applicable fees for electronic payments, not impose products on consumers, and avoid charging for services not requested. These requirements ensure transparency and ethical handling of consumer data throughout the relationship.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates ethical business practices and clear communication of fees and services, which are critical for data transfer impact assessments to ensure consumer data is handled transparently and responsibly.

requirements.text: Conduct business in a responsible, professional and ethical manner
Train staff to promote competence, efficiency and professionalism in discharge of duties
Provide clear information about products and services, features, terms and conditions and applicable fees and charges
Provide and inform consumers of channels to make enquiries and complaints
Notify customers of circumstances that may affect terms of contracts or relationships
Display all applicable fees for electronic payment services with option to accept or decline before transaction
Not impose any product, service or channel on consumers
Not charge fees for issuance and renewal of products or services which have not been requested by the customer
Not compel consumers to buy products or services as a condition for another product unless directly dependent

sections.source_quote: Institutions shall comply with the following provisions: Conduct their business in a responsible, professional and ethical manner.

sections.title: Complaints handling and redress mechanisms
sections.summary: Institutions must establish complaint channels, internal dispute resolution processes, and external escalation to the Central Bank of Nigeria. They must respond to consumer inquiries within five working days and provide timely resolution for complaints, ensuring fair and transparent redress mechanisms for financial consumers.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires institutions to have structured complaint handling processes that impact data transfer impact assessments by ensuring consumer data disputes are resolved transparently and efficiently.

requirements.text: Establish complaints management channels for consumers
Implement internal dispute resolution processes
Provide dispute resolution at the Central Bank of Nigeria for unresolved cases
Respond to customers' enquiries/requests within five (5) working days
Communicate decisions on waivers, extensions, and concessions within ten (10) working days

sections.source_quote: Institutions shall establish complaints management channels, internal dispute resolution processes, and external escalation to the Central Bank of Nigeria.

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Consumer data privacy and consent requirements for financial institutions
sections.summary: Financial institutions must obtain written consumer consent for specific data processing purposes and allow withdrawal of consent at any time. They must protect consumer information against unauthorized access and be accountable for related actions. Institutions must not transfer consumer data to third parties without express consent, except where legally required. They must inform consumers when data is shared with authorized third parties and review data processing procedures to ensure consent purposes remain valid.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires explicit consumer consent for data processing, prohibits unauthorized third-party data transfers without consent, mandates data sharing transparency, and necessitates periodic review of data processing purposes – all critical for cross-border data transfers involving Nigerian financial institutions.

requirements.text: Obtain the written consent of consumers to collect and process their personal data for specific purpose and provide them with the option to withdraw the consent at any time.
Protect the privacy and confidentiality of consumer information and assets against unauthorized access, and be accountable for acts or omissions in respect thereof.
Not transfer personal data of consumers to a third party without their express consent, except in compliance with a legal obligation.
Inform consumers whenever their data is exchanged with an authorized third party, stating details of the exchange.
Review data processing and privacy procedures to ensure that the purpose(s) for which initial consent was granted remained valid.
Keep accurate and updated data of consumers always.

sections.source_quote: To ensure data protection and privacy of consumers, Institutions shall: 5.4.1 Protect the privacy and confidentiality of consumer information and assets against unauthorized access, and be accountable for acts or omissions in respect thereof.

sections.heading: (continued)
sections.title: Complaint resolution reporting and outsourcing requirements
sections.summary: The RCAR must detail complaint frequency, root causes, and remedial actions. Institutions must report complaint statistics annually in financial statements per CBN format. Outsourcing complaints handling requires CBN approval. These obligations apply to financial institutions regulated by the Central Bank of Nigeria.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires institutions to document complaint resolution processes and report statistics, impacting data transfer assessments for complaint handling systems and third-party outsourcing.

requirements.text: The RCAR shall contain the frequency of occurrence, identified root cause(s) and the remedial actions implemented.
Report complaints statistics in annual reports and the abridged Financial Statement in line with the approved format or any other format as may be prescribed by the CBN from time to time.
Institutions may outsource complaints handling functions subject to CBN approval.

sections.source_quote: 6.3.17 The RCAR shall contain the frequency of occurrence, identified root cause(s) and the remedial actions implemented.

sections.heading: below:
sections.title: Penalties for non-compliance with complaint resolution
sections.summary: Institutions face penalties for failing to resolve complaints within timelines, acknowledging complaints, issuing tracking numbers, responding to requests, or complying with CBN directives. Penalties include fines up to N500,000 per week for unresolved complaints, N2,000,000 per complaint for non-acknowledgment, and additional sanctions for persistent breaches.
sections.dtia_summary: Imposes financial penalties for non-compliance with complaint resolution processes, directly impacting data transfer assessments for complaint handling systems.

requirements.text: A penalty of N500,000.00 per complaint per week while the infraction subsists.
Non-acknowledgment of complaints from customer or non-issuance of tracking numbers – N2,000,000.00 per complaint.
Non-response to request or failure to comply with CBN directive - A penalty of N2,000,000.00.
False or non-rendition of Returns/Reports - N100,000.00, and in addition, N10,000 for each day the infraction continues.
Persistent breach of regulations - Administrative sanctions on responsible officer(s), including issuance of warning letters and any other statutory sanctions on the officer(s) or Institution.

sections.source_quote: 7.1 Non-resolution of complaints within prescribed timelines - A penalty of N500,000.00 per complaint per week while the infraction subsists.

sections.heading: below: (cont.)
sections.title: Timelines for complaint resolution by financial institutions
sections.summary: This section specifies standardized timelines for resolving customer complaints across various financial services categories. It outlines the number of working days required for first and second reviews for each complaint type, including account management, cards, loans, fraud, and international transactions. The timelines apply to all financial institutions regulated under the Consumer Protection Regulations.

requirements.text: Account Management: 2 working days for first review and 1 working day for second review
Card Activation: 3 working days for first review and 2 working days for second review
Loan Application Issues: 14 working days for first review and 7 working days for second review
Fraudulent Withdrawals: 14 working days for first review and 7 working days for second review
International Banking Fraud (Not on us): 60 working days for first review and 30 working days for second review
International Banking Fraud (on us): 50 working days for first review and 25 working days for second review

sections.source_quote: TIMELINES FOR COMPLAINTS RESOLUTION BY INSTITUTIONS: Category Sub-category CCMS Code 1st Review (working days) 2nd review (working days)
